steps.title: Use the height formula
steps.content:
For an equilateral triangle with side length $s$, the height is
$$h=\frac{s\sqrt{3}}{2}$$
The given height is $49\sqrt{3}$.

steps.title: Solve for the side length
steps.content:
Set the formula equal to the given height and solve.
$$49\sqrt{3}=\frac{s\sqrt{3}}{2}$$
$$98\sqrt{3}=s\sqrt{3}$$
$$s=98$$
The answer is B.
